name: Tree of Life
shortDescription: The Tree of Life in Tarot is a diagram representing the structure of the cosmos and the path to enlightenment, often depicted with ten Sephirot and interconnected paths corresponding to Major Arcana cards.
description: The Tree of Life, derived from Jewish Kabbalah, serves as a foundational structure in Tarot. It represents the emanations of the divine and the interconnectedness of all things. In the Tarot context, the ten Sephirot (spheres) symbolize different aspects of God's nature and stages of creation, while the twenty-two paths connecting them are often associated with the twenty-two Major Arcana cards. Each Sephirah embodies specific qualities and archetypes, offering a map for spiritual exploration and self-discovery. Understanding the Tree of Life provides a deeper understanding of the Major Arcana, as it illuminates the underlying principles and energetic flows represented by each card. The Tree acts as both a symbolic representation of the cosmos and a practical tool for personal growth, meditation, and divination. By studying the Sephirot and their corresponding Tarot cards, individuals can gain insights into their own psyche, navigate life's challenges, and ultimately achieve a higher state of consciousness. The arrangement of the Sephirot, from Kether (the Crown) at the top to Malkuth (the Kingdom) at the bottom, illustrates a descending flow of divine energy into the material world, as well as an ascending path of spiritual ascent back to the divine source. This system is central to Hermetic Qabalah and Western esoteric traditions and is commonly used in conjunction with astrology, alchemy, and other occult sciences.

sources.title: 777 and Other Qabalistic Writings of Aleister Crowley
sources.author: Aleister Crowley
